description: Web-based dashboard for real-time agent monitoring, AI chat interface, and agent controls

issues.title: [META] Project Progress Tracker
issues.description: Session handoff and project coordination issue. Use this issue to track overall progress, blockers, and session context.

issues.title: Phase 1: Foundation - Dashboard Server & Agent Status
issues.description:
Phase 1: Foundation
Goal: Dashboard server starts and serves a static page with agent status.
Success criteria: `python scripts/dashboard_server.py --project-dir my-app` opens a browser showing all 13 agents with their current status from `.agent_metrics.json`.

Test Steps:
1. Start dashboard server
2. Navigate to http://localhost:8420
3. Verify all 13 agents display with current status
4. Verify status updates when refreshing page
5. Verify API endpoints respond correctly

Deliverables:
- Create `scripts/dashboard_server.py` with async HTTP server
- Create single-file HTML dashboard with agent status panel
- REST API: `/api/health`, `/api/metrics`, `/api/agents`
- Load data from existing `MetricsStore`

issues.title: Phase 2: Real-Time Updates - WebSocket & Event Streaming
issues.description:
Phase 2: Real-Time Updates
Goal: Agent status updates appear in the dashboard without page refresh.
Success criteria: When the orchestrator delegates to a coding agent, the dashboard shows the agent transition from Idle to Running within 1 second.

Test Steps:
1. Start orchestrator with dashboard server running
2. Observe agent status changes in real-time
3. Verify WebSocket connections establish and maintain
4. Test reconnection logic on disconnect
5. Verify activity feed updates in real-time

Deliverables:
- Add WebSocket server
- Add event emission hooks in `agent.py` and `agents/orchestrator.py`
- Frontend WebSocket client with auto-reconnection
- Live activity feed

issues.title: Phase 3: AI Chat Interface - Multi-Provider Support
issues.description:
Phase 3: AI Chat Interface
Goal: User can chat with Claude through the dashboard and see responses.
Success criteria: User types "What is the status of all Linear issues?" and receives a formatted response with data from Linear.

Test Steps:
1. Open dashboard chat interface
2. Type message
3. Verify message appears in history
4. Verify AI response streams in real-time
5. Verify code blocks render with syntax highlighting
6. Verify Linear/GitHub/Slack tool calls are shown

Deliverables:
- Chat UI component (message thread, input box)
- POST `/api/chat` endpoint with streaming response
- Provider/model selector UI
- Tool call transparency (show Linear/Slack/GitHub tool invocations)

issues.title: Phase 4: Multi-Provider Switching - Provider Detection & Hot-Swap
issues.description:
Phase 4: Multi-Provider Support
Goal: User can switch AI providers and models in the chat.
Success criteria: User switches from Claude to Gemini, sends a message, and receives a response from Gemini 2.5 Flash.

Test Steps:
1. Verify all 6 providers display in selector
2. Check provider availability indicators (Available/Unconfigured/Error)
3. Switch between providers without losing chat history
4. Verify correct model appears in selector for each provider
5. Test hot-swap during conversation

Deliverables:
- Provider availability detection (check API keys)
- Bridge module integration for chat routing
- Provider status indicators in UI
- Hot-swap without context loss

issues.title: Phase 5: Agent Controls - Pause, Resume & Requirement Editing
issues.description:
Phase 5: Agent Controls
Goal: User can pause, edit requirements, and resume agents.
Success criteria: User pauses the coding agent, edits the requirement for AI-42, resumes, and the agent picks up the updated requirements.

Test Steps:
1. Click pause on running agent
2. Verify agent stops accepting new delegations
3. Open requirement editor
4. Modify requirement text
5. Save changes
6. Resume agent
7. Verify agent uses updated requirements

Deliverables:
- Pause/Resume API endpoints and UI controls
- Requirement editor UI
- Requirement sync to Linear (optional)
- Pause All / Resume All global controls

issues.title: Phase 6: Transparency Features - Reasoning, Decisions & Code Streaming
issues.description:
Phase 6: Reasoning and Decision Transparency
Goal: User can see why the orchestrator made each decision.
Success criteria: When the orchestrator delegates to `coding` instead of `coding_fast`, the dashboard shows the reasoning: "Complexity: COMPLEX — auth keyword found. Routing to coding (sonnet)."

Test Steps:
1. Observe orchestrator reasoning in chat
2. Verify complexity assessment displays
3. Expand/collapse reasoning blocks
4. Watch live code streaming from coding agents
5. View file change summary after delegation
6. Verify test results display

Deliverables:
- Reasoning event emission from orchestrator
- Collapsible reasoning blocks in chat UI
- Decision history log
- Live code streaming from coding agents

issues.title: REQ-CHAT-001: Implement Conversational Chat Interface
issues.parent: Phase 3: AI Chat Interface - Multi-Provider Support
issues.description:
REQ-CHAT-001: Conversational Interface
The dashboard must provide a chat interface where users type natural language messages and receive responses from the AI system. Messages display in a scrollable conversation thread with clear visual distinction between user messages and AI responses.

Test Steps:
1. Load dashboard and locate chat interface
2. Type a test message
3. Verify message appears in user message format
4. Observe AI response streams in
5. Verify response displays in AI message format
6. Scroll through conversation history
7. Verify visual distinction between user/AI messages

Acceptance Criteria:
- Messages display in scrollable thread
- User messages clearly distinguished from AI responses
- Chat interface is responsive
- No external dependencies required

issues.title: REQ-CHAT-002: Implement Message History Persistence
issues.parent: Phase 3: AI Chat Interface - Multi-Provider Support
issues.description:
REQ-CHAT-002: Message History
The chat must persist message history for the current session. Messages include:
- User input (text)
- AI response (text, code blocks, structured data)
- System messages (agent status changes, errors, notifications)
- Timestamps for all messages

Test Steps:
1. Send multiple messages to chat
2. Verify all messages retain order
3. Check timestamps are accurate
4. Verify system messages appear correctly
5. Refresh page and verify history persists
6. Test history limit (maintain last 100 messages)

Acceptance Criteria:
- All message types persist
- Timestamps are accurate
- History survives page refresh
- Performance is acceptable with large history

issues.title: REQ-CHAT-003: Implement Code Block Rendering with Syntax Highlighting
issues.parent: Phase 3: AI Chat Interface - Multi-Provider Support
issues.description:
REQ-CHAT-003: Code Block Rendering
AI responses containing code must render with:
- Syntax highlighting appropriate to the language
- Copy-to-clipboard button
- File path annotation when the code references a specific file
- Diff view for code changes (additions in green, deletions in red)

Test Steps:
1. Request code from AI (e.g., "Write a Python function")
2. Verify syntax highlighting applies to code block
3. Click copy button and paste in editor
4. Verify file path displays if provided
5. Request code diff and verify colors (green/red)
6. Test multiple language syntax highlighting (Python, JS, etc.)

Acceptance Criteria:
- Syntax highlighting works for major languages
- Copy button works correctly
- File paths display clearly
- Diffs show with color coding

issues.title: REQ-PROVIDER-001: Implement Provider Switcher UI
issues.parent: Phase 4: Multi-Provider Switching - Provider Detection & Hot-Swap
issues.description:
REQ-PROVIDER-001: Provider Switcher
The chat interface must include a provider selector that allows the user to switch between available AI providers. Supported providers:
- Claude (default) — Haiku 4.5, Sonnet 4.5, Opus 4.6
- ChatGPT — GPT-4o, o1, o3-mini, o4-mini
- Gemini — 2.5 Flash, 2.5 Pro, 2.0 Flash
- Groq — Llama 3.3 70B, Mixtral 8x7B
- KIMI — Moonshot (2M context)
- Windsurf — Cascade

Test Steps:
1. Locate provider selector in UI
2. Verify all 6 providers appear in dropdown
3. Click each provider and verify it highlights
4. Verify default provider is Claude
5. Send message with different provider selected
6. Verify response comes from selected provider

Acceptance Criteria:
- All 6 providers available in selector
- Current selection displays
- Selection persists across messages
- UI is intuitive and accessible

issues.title: REQ-PROVIDER-002: Implement Model Selector with Provider-Specific Models
issues.parent: Phase 4: Multi-Provider Switching - Provider Detection & Hot-Swap
issues.description:
REQ-PROVIDER-002: Model Selector
Within each provider, the user must be able to select a specific model. The selector must:
- Show only models available for the selected provider
- Display the currently active model
- Persist the selection for the duration of the session
- Default to the provider's recommended model

Test Steps:
1. Select Claude provider
2. Verify Claude models appear (Haiku, Sonnet, Opus)
3. Select Gemini provider
4. Verify model list updates to Gemini models
5. Select a specific model (e.g., Sonnet)
6. Verify model persists on next message
7. Switch providers and back
8. Verify model selection is preserved per provider

Acceptance Criteria:
- Model list updates dynamically with provider
- Selection persists during session
- Defaults are sensible (Sonnet for Claude, etc.)
- Cascade of dropdowns is smooth

issues.title: REQ-PROVIDER-003: Implement Provider Status Indicators
issues.parent: Phase 4: Multi-Provider Switching - Provider Detection & Hot-Swap
issues.description:
REQ-PROVIDER-003: Provider Status Indicators
Each provider in the selector must show its availability status:
- Available — API key configured, provider reachable
- Unconfigured — API key missing (show setup instructions on hover)
- Error — API key present but provider unreachable

Test Steps:
1. Check provider selector for status indicators
2. Verify icons/colors for Available status (green)
3. Verify icons/colors for Unconfigured status (gray)
4. Hover over Unconfigured provider
5. Verify setup instructions appear
6. Simulate provider error (disconnect network)
7. Verify Error status displays (red)

Acceptance Criteria:
- Status indicators display correctly
- Colors are intuitive (green/gray/red)
- Setup instructions are helpful
- Status updates dynamically

issues.title: REQ-PROVIDER-004: Implement Hot-Swap Without Context Loss
issues.parent: Phase 4: Multi-Provider Switching - Provider Detection & Hot-Swap
issues.description:
REQ-PROVIDER-004: Hot-Swap Without Context Loss
Switching providers mid-conversation must:
- Preserve the full conversation history in the UI
- Insert a system message noting the provider change
- Send conversation context to the new provider for continuity
- Not interrupt any running agent operations

Test Steps:
1. Start conversation with Claude
2. Send 3-4 messages
3. Switch to Gemini provider
4. Verify all previous messages still visible
5. Check system message about provider change appears
6. Send message with Gemini
7. Verify context was passed (AI references previous messages)
8. Switch back to Claude
9. Verify previous context is maintained

Acceptance Criteria:
- Chat history fully preserved
- System message insertion is clear
- Context is passed to new provider
- No message loss or reordering

issues.title: REQ-INTEGRATION-001: Implement Linear Access in Chat
issues.parent: Phase 3: AI Chat Interface - Multi-Provider Support
issues.description:
REQ-INTEGRATION-001: Linear Access
The chat interface must have access to all 39 Linear MCP tools defined in `scripts/arcade_config.py`. Users can:
- Query issue status ("What's the status of AI-42?")
- Create issues ("Create a bug for the login page crash")
- Transition issues ("Move AI-42 to In Progress")
- View project boards and backlogs

Test Steps:
1. Ask chat "What is the status of AI-1?"
2. Verify Linear tool is called
3. Verify response contains issue data
4. Request to create a test issue via chat
5. Verify issue is created in Linear
6. Check that multiple tool calls work
7. Verify tool responses integrate into chat response

Acceptance Criteria:
- All Linear tools accessible through chat
- Tool calls succeed and return data
- User-friendly natural language commands work
- Error handling is graceful

issues.title: REQ-INTEGRATION-002: Implement Slack Access in Chat
issues.parent: Phase 3: AI Chat Interface - Multi-Provider Support
issues.description:
REQ-INTEGRATION-002: Slack Access
The chat interface must have access to all 8 Slack MCP tools. Users can:
- Send messages to configured channels
- Read recent messages
- React to messages
- Pin important updates

Test Steps:
1. Request to send message to #notifications
2. Verify message appears in Slack
3. Read recent messages via chat
4. Verify messages display in chat
5. Request to react to a message
6. Verify reaction appears in Slack
7. Pin a message via chat
8. Verify pin appears in Slack

Acceptance Criteria:
- All Slack operations work
- Messages sync correctly
- No rate limiting issues
- Error handling for missing channels

issues.title: REQ-INTEGRATION-003: Implement GitHub Access in Chat
issues.parent: Phase 3: AI Chat Interface - Multi-Provider Support
issues.description:
REQ-INTEGRATION-003: GitHub Access
The chat interface must have access to all 46 GitHub MCP tools. Users can:
- View PR status and diffs
- Create and merge PRs
- View repository information
- Manage issues and labels

Test Steps:
1. Ask "What's the status of PR #42?"
2. Verify PR data returns
3. Request PR diff and verify it displays
4. Ask to create a PR via chat
5. Verify PR is created on GitHub
6. Check repository information queries work
7. Test issue/label management commands

Acceptance Criteria:
- All GitHub tools accessible
- PR diffs render correctly
- Create/merge operations work
- Repository queries complete successfully

issues.title: REQ-INTEGRATION-004: Implement Tool Transparency & Call Visualization
issues.parent: Phase 3: AI Chat Interface - Multi-Provider Support
issues.description:
REQ-INTEGRATION-004: Tool Transparency
When the AI invokes an external tool (Linear, Slack, GitHub), the chat must:
- Show which tool is being called and with what parameters (collapsible detail)
- Show the tool's response (collapsible detail)
- Indicate success or failure with a visual status icon

Test Steps:
1. Request action that calls Linear tool
2. Verify tool name appears in chat
3. Click to expand tool parameters
4. Verify parameters display correctly
5. Check tool response is shown
6. Verify success icon appears (green checkmark)
7. Simulate tool error
8. Verify error icon and message appear
9. Verify tool blocks are collapsible

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tool calls are visible
- Parameters can be inspected
- Responses are shown
- Status icons are clear
- All blocks are collapsible

issues.title: REQ-MONITOR-001: Implement Agent Status Panel
issues.parent: Phase 1: Foundation - Dashboard Server & Agent Status
issues.description:
REQ-MONITOR-001: Agent Status Panel
The dashboard must display a panel showing all 13 agents defined in `agents/definitions.py` with their current status:
- Running: Green pulsing dot
- Idle: Gray dot
- Paused: Yellow dot
- Error: Red dot

Test Steps:
1. Load dashboard and find agent status panel
2. Verify all 13 agents appear
3. Click on running agent
4. Verify green pulsing dot shows
5. Check idle agents have gray dots
6. Pause an agent and verify yellow dot
7. Simulate error and verify red dot
8. Verify status updates in real-time

Acceptance Criteria:
- All 13 agents listed
- Status indicators are accurate
- Colors are distinct and intuitive
- Real-time updates work
issues.component_reference: TaskCard for agent status display, ProgressRing for visual indicators

issues.title: REQ-MONITOR-002: Implement Active Requirement Display
issues.parent: Phase 1: Foundation - Dashboard Server & Agent Status
issues.description:
REQ-MONITOR-002: Active Requirement Display
When an agent is in "Running" status, the panel must show:
- The Linear ticket key and title the agent is working on (e.g., "AI-42: Add user authentication")
- The full requirement description (expandable)
- Time elapsed since the agent started this delegation
- Token count accumulating in real-time
- Estimated cost accumulating in real-time

Test Steps:
1. Start orchestrator with dashboard
2. Observe running agent
3. Verify ticket key and title display
4. Click to expand requirement description
5. Verify full description appears
6. Check timer increments correctly
7. Verify token count updates in real-time
8. Verify cost calculation is accurate

Acceptance Criteria:
- Ticket info displays clearly
- Expandable description works
- Timer is accurate
- Token/cost tracking is real-time
- Numbers format clearly (K for thousands, etc.)

issues.title: REQ-MONITOR-003: Implement Agent Detail View
issues.parent: Phase 2: Real-Time Updates - WebSocket & Event Streaming
issues.description:
REQ-MONITOR-003: Agent Detail View
Clicking on an agent in the status panel opens a detail view showing:
- The agent's `AgentProfile` data (from `metrics.py` types):
  - Lifetime statistics (invocations, success rate, tokens, cost)
  - Contribution counters (commits, PRs, files, issues, messages, reviews)
  - Gamification data (XP, level, streak, achievements)
  - Strengths and weaknesses (auto-detected)
- Recent event history (last 20 events from `recent_events`)
- The agent's current model assignment (from `DEFAULT_MODELS` in `definitions.py`)

Test Steps:
1. Click on an agent in status panel
2. Verify detail panel opens
3. Check lifetime statistics display
4. Verify contribution counters show
5. Check gamification data (XP, level, streak)
6. Verify strengths/weaknesses appear
7. Scroll recent event history
8. Verify last 20 events display
9. Check model assignment shows
10. Close detail view

Acceptance Criteria:
- All agent profile data displays
- Event history loads correctly
- Detail panel is readable
- Close functionality works

issues.title: REQ-MONITOR-004: Implement Orchestrator Flow Visualization
issues.parent: Phase 2: Real-Time Updates - WebSocket & Event Streaming
issues.description:
REQ-MONITOR-004: Orchestrator Flow Visualization
The dashboard must show the orchestrator's current delegation flow:
```
Orchestrator
  ├─► ops (Starting AI-42) ✓ 3s
  ├─► coding (Implementing AI-42) ● 45s [ACTIVE]
  ├─► github (Commit + PR) ○ [PENDING]
  └─► ops (Mark Done + Notify) ○ [PENDING]
```

This visualizes the pipeline defined in the orchestrator prompt.

Test Steps:
1. Start orchestrator with dashboard
2. Observe flow visualization
3. Verify current active step highlights
4. Check completed steps show checkmarks
5. Verify pending steps show circles
6. Monitor time elapsed per step
7. Verify flow updates as agents complete
8. Check flow clears when orchestrator finishes

Acceptance Criteria:
- Flow structure matches orchestrator pipeline
- Status indicators are correct
- Timing is accurate
- Visual hierarchy is clear
issues.component_reference: FileTree for hierarchical flow display, ProgressRing for status indicators

issues.title: REQ-METRICS-001: Implement Global Metrics Bar
issues.parent: Phase 2: Real-Time Updates - WebSocket & Event Streaming
issues.description:
REQ-METRICS-001: Global Metrics Bar
The top of the dashboard must show global metrics (from `DashboardState` in `metrics.py`):
- Total sessions completed
- Total tokens consumed
- Total estimated cost (USD)
- Total uptime/duration
- Current session number

Test Steps:
1. Load dashboard
2. Check header for global metrics
3. Verify sessions count displays
4. Check total tokens show
5. Verify cost displays in USD
6. Check uptime/duration shows
7. Verify current session number
8. Run agent delegation
9. Verify metrics update in real-time
10. Check formatting is readable

Acceptance Criteria:
- All 5 metrics display
- Updates are real-time
- Formatting is clear and readable
- Metrics match `DashboardState` data

issues.title: REQ-METRICS-002: Implement Agent Leaderboard
issues.parent: Phase 2: Real-Time Updates - WebSocket & Event Streaming
issues.description:
REQ-METRICS-002: Agent Leaderboard
Display agents ranked by XP (from `AgentProfile.xp`), showing:
- Rank
- Agent name
- Level and XP
- Success rate
- Average execution time
- Total cost
- Current status (Running/Idle/Paused/Error)

Test Steps:
1. Locate leaderboard in footer or sidebar
2. Verify agents ranked by XP
3. Check rank numbers display correctly
4. Verify all agent names appear
5. Check level and XP show correctly
6. Verify success rates calculate
7. Check execution time averages
8. Verify total cost displays
9. Check status icons appear
10. Verify leaderboard updates in real-time

Acceptance Criteria:
- All columns display
- Ranking is accurate
- Data is current
- Status indicators match agent status
- Leaderboard is sortable (by XP, cost, success rate, etc.)
issues.component_reference: TaskCard for agent rows with metrics

issues.title: REQ-METRICS-003: Implement Cost and Token Charts
issues.parent: Phase 2: Real-Time Updates - WebSocket & Event Streaming
issues.description:
REQ-METRICS-003: Cost and Token Charts
Provide visual charts for:
- Token usage by agent (horizontal bar chart)
- Cost trend over recent sessions (line chart)
- Success rate by agent (bar chart)

Test Steps:
1. Find charts section in dashboard
2. Verify token usage bar chart displays
3. Check agents ranked by tokens
4. Verify cost trend line chart
5. Check time period selector works
6. Verify success rate bar chart
7. Confirm bars are proportional
8. Test chart interactions (hover, zoom, etc.)
9. Verify charts update with new data

Acceptance Criteria:
- All 3 charts render correctly
- Data is accurate
- Charts are readable and proportional
- Charts update in real-time
- Mobile responsive behavior

issues.title: REQ-FEED-001: Implement Live Activity Feed
issues.parent: Phase 2: Real-Time Updates - WebSocket & Event Streaming
issues.description:
REQ-FEED-001: Live Activity Feed
A scrolling feed showing agent events as they happen:
- Timestamp
- Agent name
- Status icon (success/error)
- Ticket key
- Duration
- Brief description

Test Steps:
1. Locate activity feed in left sidebar
2. Verify feed displays events in chronological order
3. Check timestamps are accurate
4. Verify agent names appear
5. Check status icons (green for success, red for error)
6. Verify ticket keys display
7. Check duration shows (e.g., "2m 34s")
8. Verify brief description is helpful
9. Scroll up to see older events
10. Verify new events appear at top

Acceptance Criteria:
- Events display in real-time
- Latest events appear at top
- All fields display correctly
- Timestamps are accurate
- Feed handles many events without lag

issues.title: REQ-CONTROL-001: Implement Pause Agent Control
issues.parent: Phase 5: Agent Controls - Pause, Resume & Requirement Editing
issues.description:
REQ-CONTROL-001: Pause Agent
The user must be able to pause a running agent. Pausing:
- Signals the orchestrator to stop delegating new work to this agent
- Does NOT terminate an in-progress delegation (the current task completes)
- Sets the agent's status to "Paused" in the status panel
- Inserts a system message in the chat: "Agent [name] paused by user"
- Prevents the orchestrator from selecting this agent for new tasks

Test Steps:
1. Locate running agent in status panel
2. Click pause button
3. Verify yellow dot appears (Paused status)
4. Check system message in chat
5. Verify current delegation continues
6. Watch delegation complete
7. Verify new delegations skip this agent
8. Confirm orchestrator respects pause state

Acceptance Criteria:
- Pause works on running agents
- Status changes to Paused
- System message is clear
- Current work completes
- New work is blocked

issues.title: REQ-CONTROL-002: Implement Resume Agent Control
issues.parent: Phase 5: Agent Controls - Pause, Resume & Requirement Editing
issues.description:
REQ-CONTROL-002: Resume Agent
The user must be able to resume a paused agent. Resuming:
- Restores the agent to "Idle" status
- Makes the agent available for new delegations
- Inserts a system message in the chat: "Agent [name] resumed by user"

Test Steps:
1. Pause a running agent
2. Verify Paused status shows
3. Click resume button
4. Verify status changes to Idle
5. Check system message appears
6. Wait for new delegation
7. Verify agent accepts new work
8. Check resume works on idle agents too

Acceptance Criteria:
- Resume changes status to Idle
- System message is clear
- Agent accepts new work
- Resume works on paused agents

issues.title: REQ-CONTROL-003: Implement Pause All / Resume All Controls
issues.parent: Phase 5: Agent Controls - Pause, Resume & Requirement Editing
issues.description:
REQ-CONTROL-003: Pause All / Resume All
Global controls to pause or resume all agents simultaneously. When all agents are paused:
- The orchestrator enters a holding state
- A prominent banner shows "All agents paused — system is idle"
- No new delegations are dispatched

Test Steps:
1. Locate "Pause All" button in footer
2. Click "Pause All"
3. Verify banner appears
4. Check all agents have yellow dots
5. Verify orchestrator stops delegating
6. Click "Resume All"
7. Verify banner disappears
8. Check agents return to Idle
9. Verify orchestrator resumes delegating

Acceptance Criteria:
- Pause All works instantly
- Resume All works instantly
- Banner is prominent and clear
- Orchestrator respects global pause state
issues.component_reference: ApprovalCard for confirmation dialog if needed

issues.title: REQ-CONTROL-004: Implement View Current Requirements
issues.parent: Phase 5: Agent Controls - Pause, Resume & Requirement Editing
issues.description:
REQ-CONTROL-004: View Current Requirements
The dashboard must show the current requirement instructions for each active ticket. Requirements come from:
- Linear issue title and description (via Linear MCP tools)
- The `app_spec.txt` specification (from `specs/`)
- Any additional context passed by the orchestrator

Test Steps:
1. Identify running agent with active ticket
2. Click on active requirement display
3. Verify ticket title shows
4. Check full requirement description appears
5. Verify source is noted (Linear/spec/context)
6. Test with multiple tickets
7. Verify expandable/collapsible sections work

Acceptance Criteria:
- All requirement sources visible
- Description is complete
- Source attribution is clear
- View works for all agents

issues.title: REQ-CONTROL-005: Implement Edit Requirements Mid-Flight
issues.parent: Phase 5: Agent Controls - Pause, Resume & Requirement Editing
issues.description:
REQ-CONTROL-005: Edit Requirements Mid-Flight
While an agent is paused, the user must be able to:
- Edit the requirement text for the ticket the agent was working on
- The edit interface is a text editor with markdown support
- Changes are saved and will be passed to the agent when it resumes

Test Steps:
1. Pause a running agent
2. Click "Edit Requirements" button
3. Verify text editor appears
4. Check current requirement text is shown
5. Modify text (add/remove requirements)
6. Test markdown syntax (bold, code blocks, lists)
7. Click Save
8. Verify save succeeds
9. Resume agent
10. Verify agent uses updated requirements

Acceptance Criteria:
- Editor is intuitive
- Markdown support works
- Changes persist
- Agent uses updated text
- Undo/discard options available

issues.title: REQ-CONTROL-006: Implement Requirement Edit Flow
issues.parent: Phase 5: Agent Controls - Pause, Resume & Requirement Editing
issues.description:
REQ-CONTROL-006: Requirement Edit Flow
The complete flow for editing requirements:
1. User pauses the agent (the current delegation completes)
2. User opens the requirement editor for the agent's current ticket
3. User modifies the requirement instructions
4. User saves the changes
5. User resumes the agent
6. The orchestrator passes the updated requirements to the agent on next delegation

Test Steps:
1. Start with running agent (e.g., coding)
2. Click pause
3. Wait for current task to complete
4. Click "Edit Requirements"
5. Make modification
6. Save
7. Click "Resume"
8. Wait for next delegation
9. Verify agent starts new task
10. Check agent's generated output reflects requirement change

Acceptance Criteria:
- Flow is smooth and intuitive
- All steps execute correctly
- Agent respects updated requirements
- No data loss during edit
- Clear feedback at each step

issues.title: REQ-CONTROL-007: Implement Requirement Sync to Linear
issues.parent: Phase 5: Agent Controls - Pause, Resume & Requirement Editing
issues.description:
REQ-CONTROL-007: Requirement Sync to Linear
When the user edits requirements through the dashboard, changes must optionally sync back to the Linear issue description (configurable via a toggle). This uses the existing Linear MCP tools.

Test Steps:
1. Open requirement editor for a ticket
2. Find "Sync to Linear" toggle
3. Toggle it ON
4. Edit requirement
5. Save
6. Verify Linear issue description updates
7. Check change appears in Linear UI
8. Toggle sync OFF
9. Edit again
10. Verify Linear is NOT updated

Acceptance Criteria:
- Toggle is visible and clear
- Sync works when enabled
- No sync when disabled
- Linear updates are atomic
- Error handling if Linear API fails

issues.title: REQ-REASONING-001: Implement Live Reasoning Stream Display
issues.parent: Phase 6: Transparency Features - Reasoning, Decisions & Code Streaming
issues.description:
REQ-REASONING-001: Live Reasoning Stream
The chat interface must show the AI's reasoning and decision-making process in real-time, similar to how Claude Code displays its thinking. This includes:
- The orchestrator's decision about which agent to delegate to and why
- The orchestrator's complexity assessment (Simple vs Complex)
- Context being passed between agents

Display format:
```
┌─ Orchestrator Reasoning ─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Ticket AI-42: "Add user authentication"                       │
│ Complexity: COMPLEX (auth, security-related code)             │
│ Decision: Delegate to `coding` (sonnet) — not `coding_fast`  │
│ Reason: Security-related changes require deeper analysis      │
│ Context passed: Full issue description + existing auth files  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Test Steps:
1. Send message to orchestrator
2. Observe reasoning block appears
3. Verify reasoning content is detailed
4. Check complexity assessment
5. Verify agent selection reasoning
6. Confirm context description
7. Click to collapse reasoning
8. Verify can expand again
9. Test multiple reasoning blocks

Acceptance Criteria:
- Reasoning displays in real-time
- Content is accurate and detailed
- Blocks are collapsible
- Format matches specification
- Multiple blocks can be toggled

issues.title: REQ-REASONING-002: Implement Agent Thinking Display
issues.parent: Phase 6: Transparency Features - Reasoning, Decisions & Code Streaming
issues.description:
REQ-REASONING-002: Agent Thinking Display
When an agent is working, display its reasoning steps:
- What files it is reading and why
- What changes it is planning
- What commands it is executing
- What tests it is running

This is the "inner monologue" of the agent, streamed in real-time.

Test Steps:
1. Start agent delegation (e.g., coding agent)
2. Observe thinking block appears
3. Check file reads are listed with reasons
4. Verify planned changes show
5. Watch commands execute in real-time
6. Check test execution displays
7. Verify streaming is smooth
8. Check thinking block can collapse

Acceptance Criteria:
- Thinking displays as agent works
- All 4 categories (files, changes, commands, tests) show
- Streaming is fluid
- Content is accurate
- Block is collapsible
issues.component_reference: ActivityItem for each thinking step display

issues.title: REQ-REASONING-003: Implement Collapsible Reasoning Blocks
issues.parent: Phase 6: Transparency Features - Reasoning, Decisions & Code Streaming
issues.description:
REQ-REASONING-003: Collapsible Reasoning Blocks
Reasoning blocks must be collapsible:
- Default: collapsed, showing only a one-line summary
- Expanded: full reasoning text, tool calls, and responses
- The user can toggle individual blocks or expand/collapse all

Test Steps:
1. Load chat with reasoning blocks
2. Verify blocks default to collapsed
3. Check one-line summaries are visible
4. Click block to expand
5. Verify full content appears
6. Click to collapse again
7. Test "Expand All" button
8. Verify all blocks expand
9. Test "Collapse All" button
10. Verify all blocks collapse

Acceptance Criteria:
- Collapse state is default
- Expand/collapse toggles work
- One-line summaries are helpful
- Expand All / Collapse All buttons work
- State persists during scroll
- Smooth animations

issues.title: REQ-DECISION-001: Implement Decision Log
issues.parent: Phase 6: Transparency Features - Reasoning, Decisions & Code Streaming
issues.description:
REQ-DECISION-001: Decision Log
Maintain a log of all orchestrator decisions:
- Agent selection decisions (which agent was chosen and alternatives considered)
- Complexity assessments
- Verification gate decisions (skip vs. run verification)
- PR review routing (pr_reviewer vs pr_reviewer_fast)
- Error recovery decisions

Test Steps:
1. Run multiple delegations
2. Access decision history page/panel
3. Verify all decision types appear
4. Check agent selection decisions
5. Verify complexity assessments
6. Check verification gate decisions
7. Verify PR review routing decisions
8. Check error recovery decisions
9. Scroll through decision history
10. Verify pagination/infinite scroll

Acceptance Criteria:
- All decision types logged
- History is complete
- Decisions are viewable
- Can be exported/searched
- Performance is good with large history
issues.component_reference: ActivityItem for each decision log entry

issues.title: REQ-DECISION-002: Implement Decision Audit Trail
issues.parent: Phase 6: Transparency Features - Reasoning, Decisions & Code Streaming
issues.description:
REQ-DECISION-002: Decision Audit Trail
Each decision in the log includes:
- Timestamp
- Decision type
- Input factors (ticket key, complexity keywords found, recent verification status)
- Outcome (agent selected, model used)
- Link to the resulting agent event

Test Steps:
1. Click on a decision in the log
2. Verify timestamp is accurate
3. Check decision type is clear
4. Verify all input factors display
5. Check ticket key and keywords show
6. Verify outcome details are complete
7. Click link to agent event
8. Verify event loads
9. Check audit trail for multiple decisions
10. Verify data is consistent

Acceptance Criteria:
- All audit trail fields present
- Data is accurate and consistent
- Links work correctly
- Display is readable
- Filtering by type works

issues.title: REQ-CODE-001: Implement Live Code Streaming Display
issues.parent: Phase 6: Transparency Features - Reasoning, Decisions & Code Streaming
issues.description:
REQ-CODE-001: Live Code Streaming
When a coding agent (coding or coding_fast) is generating code, the dashboard must:
- Stream the code as it is being written (character-by-character or chunk-by-chunk)
- Show syntax highlighting
- Show which file is being edited
- Show the diff (additions/deletions) in real-time

Test Steps:
1. Trigger coding agent delegation
2. Observe code streaming block appears
3. Verify file path shows
4. Watch code appear in real-time
5. Check syntax highlighting applies
6. Verify diff shows green/red lines
7. Test scrolling in code block
8. Check copy button works
9. Verify multiple files can stream
10. Check performance with large code changes

Acceptance Criteria:
- Code streams smoothly
- Syntax highlighting is accurate
- File path is clear
- Diff colors are correct
- No lag with large changes
- Copy functionality works

issues.title: REQ-CODE-002: Implement File Change Summary
issues.parent: Phase 6: Transparency Features - Reasoning, Decisions & Code Streaming
issues.description:
REQ-CODE-002: File Change Summary
After a coding delegation completes, show a summary:
- List of files created/modified/deleted
- Line counts (added/removed)
- Diff view for each file (collapsible)

Test Steps:
1. Wait for coding agent to complete
2. Verify summary appears
3. Check files are categorized (created/modified/deleted)
4. Verify line counts are accurate
5. Click to expand file diff
6. Check diff is complete and accurate
7. Collapse diff
8. Test with multiple files
9. Verify summary is readable
10. Check export functionality if available

Acceptance Criteria:
- Summary is comprehensive
- File categorization is correct
- Line counts are accurate
- Diffs are complete
- Collapsible sections work
- Format is readable

issues.title: REQ-CODE-003: Implement Test Results Display
issues.parent: Phase 6: Transparency Features - Reasoning, Decisions & Code Streaming
issues.description:
REQ-CODE-003: Test Results Display
When the coding agent runs tests, display:
- Test command executed
- Pass/fail status for each test
- Error output for failed tests
- Screenshot evidence (if Playwright tests)

Test Steps:
1. Trigger code change requiring tests
2. Watch test execution in real-time
3. Verify test command displays
4. Check pass/fail status for each test
5. For failed tests, verify error output
6. Check for screenshot attachments
7. Verify Playwright screenshots render
8. Test with multiple test suites
9. Verify summary shows pass rate
10. Check links to full test output

Acceptance Criteria:
- Test command is clear
- Status indicators are accurate
- Error messages are helpful
- Screenshots render
- Pass rate is calculated
- Links to full output work

issues.title: REQ-TECH-001: Implement Backend Async Server
issues.parent: Phase 1: Foundation - Dashboard Server & Agent Status
issues.description:
REQ-TECH-001: Backend Server
Python async server using `aiohttp` or `FastAPI`. The server:
- Serves the web dashboard (single-page application)
- Exposes REST API endpoints for dashboard data
- Maintains WebSocket connections for real-time updates
- Interfaces with the existing orchestrator and metrics collector

Test Steps:
1. Start dashboard server with `python scripts/dashboard_server.py`
2. Verify server starts without errors
3. Access http://localhost:8420
4. Verify health check endpoint responds
5. Test REST endpoints return correct data
6. Verify WebSocket upgrade works
7. Check server handles concurrent connections
8. Verify graceful shutdown
9. Check error handling for invalid requests
10. Verify logging

Acceptance Criteria:
- Server starts and serves requests
- All endpoints respond correctly
- WebSocket connections work
- Concurrent handling is robust
- Graceful error handling
- Minimal dependencies

issues.title: REQ-TECH-002: Implement Frontend Single HTML File
issues.parent: Phase 1: Foundation - Dashboard Server & Agent Status
issues.description:
REQ-TECH-002: Frontend
Single HTML file with embedded CSS and JavaScript (no build step required). This matches the approach specified in the existing dashboard spec:
> **No frontend build step** — single HTML file with embedded CSS/JS

Test Steps:
1. Verify HTML file can be served from server
2. Check CSS loads inline
3. Verify JavaScript executes without errors
4. Test responsive design on mobile/tablet
5. Verify dark mode toggle works
6. Check performance (load time < 2 seconds)
7. Test in multiple browsers
8. Verify accessibility (keyboard navigation)
9. Check no external CDN dependencies
10. Test offline functionality if applicable

Acceptance Criteria:
- Single HTML file (no build step)
- Embedded CSS and JS
- No external dependencies
- Responsive design
- Good performance
- Accessibility compliant

issues.title: REQ-TECH-003: Implement WebSocket Protocol & Messages
issues.parent: Phase 2: Real-Time Updates - WebSocket & Event Streaming
issues.description:
REQ-TECH-003: WebSocket Protocol
Real-time updates via WebSocket. Message types:
- `agent_status` — Agent status change (idle → running → paused → error)
- `agent_event` — New agent event recorded
- `reasoning` — Orchestrator or agent reasoning text
- `code_stream` — Live code generation chunks
- `chat_message` — Chat response chunk (streaming)
- `metrics_update` — Updated dashboard metrics
- `control_ack` — Acknowledgment of pause/resume/edit commands

Test Steps:
1. Connect WebSocket client
2. Verify initial state message
3. Simulate agent status change
4. Verify `agent_status` message received
5. Trigger agent event
6. Verify `agent_event` message
7. Test reasoning message format
8. Verify code stream chunks
9. Test chat message streaming
10. Check metrics update messages
11. Test control acknowledgments
12. Verify message ordering

Acceptance Criteria:
- All message types work
- Messages have correct format
- Order is maintained
- No message loss
- Latency < 100ms

issues.title: REQ-TECH-004: Implement REST API Endpoints
issues.parent: Phase 1: Foundation - Dashboard Server & Agent Status
issues.description:
REQ-TECH-004: REST API Endpoints
Implement all endpoints:
- GET `/api/health` — Health check
- GET `/api/metrics` — Current `DashboardState`
- GET `/api/agents` — All agent profiles
- GET `/api/agents/{name}` — Single agent profile
- GET `/api/agents/{name}/events` — Recent events for an agent
- GET `/api/sessions` — Session history
- GET `/api/providers` — Available AI providers and models
- POST `/api/chat` — Send a chat message (returns streaming response)
- POST `/api/agents/{name}/pause` — Pause an agent
- POST `/api/agents/{name}/resume` — Resume an agent
- PUT `/api/requirements/{ticket_key}` — Update requirement instructions
- GET `/api/requirements/{ticket_key}` — Get current requirement instructions
- GET `/api/decisions` — Decision history log
- GET `/` — Serve the dashboard HTML

Test Steps:
1. Test each endpoint for correct response
2. Verify data structure matches spec
3. Test error handling (404, 500, etc.)
4. Verify authentication if configured
5. Check pagination for large datasets
6. Test request validation
7. Verify response codes are correct
8. Test concurrent requests
9. Check streaming responses
10. Verify CORS headers if needed

Acceptance Criteria:
- All endpoints implemented
- Responses match specification
- Error handling is robust
- Performance is acceptable
- Documentation is clear

issues.title: REQ-TECH-005: Implement Metrics Data Source Integration
issues.parent: Phase 1: Foundation - Dashboard Server & Agent Status
issues.description:
REQ-TECH-005: Data Source
All metrics data reads from `.agent_metrics.json` via the existing `MetricsStore` class. Agent definitions come from `agents/definitions.py`. Provider availability comes from environment variable checks (API keys for each bridge module).

Test Steps:
1. Verify `.agent_metrics.json` exists and loads
2. Check MetricsStore integration works
3. Verify metrics update as agents work
4. Test provider availability detection
5. Check all 6 providers are detected
6. Verify API key checks work
7. Test with missing API keys
8. Verify agent definitions load from `definitions.py`
9. Check all 13 agents appear
10. Test data consistency

Acceptance Criteria:
- MetricsStore integration works
- All providers detected
- All agents loaded
- Data is current and consistent
- No hardcoded data

issues.title: REQ-TECH-006: Implement Metrics Collector Hook
issues.parent: Phase 2: Real-Time Updates - WebSocket & Event Streaming
issues.description:
REQ-TECH-006: Metrics Collector Hook
The dashboard server subscribes to `AgentMetricsCollector` events. When the collector records a new event (in `_record_event()` in `agent_metrics_collector.py`), it must also emit the event to connected WebSocket clients.

Test Steps:
1. Verify dashboard subscribes to metrics collector
2. Trigger agent event
3. Verify collector records event
4. Check WebSocket broadcasts event
5. Test with multiple clients
6. Verify all clients receive event
7. Test with no connected clients
8. Verify collector still works
9. Test reconnecting client gets backlog
10. Check event ordering is maintained

Acceptance Criteria:
- Metrics collector hook integrated
- Events broadcast to all clients
- No event loss
- Backlog available on reconnect
- Performance is good

issues.title: REQ-TECH-007: Implement Orchestrator Hook for Events
issues.parent: Phase 6: Transparency Features - Reasoning, Decisions & Code Streaming
issues.description:
REQ-TECH-007: Orchestrator Hook
The orchestrator (`agents/orchestrator.py`) must emit reasoning and delegation decisions to the dashboard server. This requires adding event emission points in `run_orchestrated_session()`.

Test Steps:
1. Start orchestrator with dashboard
2. Observe reasoning events emit
3. Check decision events emit
4. Verify events have correct format
5. Test event ordering
6. Verify all decision types emit
7. Check no performance impact
8. Test with multiple sessions
9. Verify graceful fallback if dashboard unavailable
10. Check event data completeness

Acceptance Criteria:
- Orchestrator emits events
- Events have all required data
- No performance impact
- Graceful fallback works
- Minimal code changes to orchestrator

issues.title: REQ-TECH-008: Implement Chat-to-Agent Bridge
issues.parent: Phase 3: AI Chat Interface - Multi-Provider Support
issues.description:
REQ-TECH-008: Chat-to-Agent Bridge
When the user sends a chat message that requires agent action, the dashboard server must:
1. Parse the user intent
2. Route to the appropriate agent (or let the orchestrator decide)
3. Execute the delegation through the existing session loop
4. Stream results back to the chat

Test Steps:
1. Send chat message requesting agent action
2. Verify intent parsing works
3. Check routing decision is correct
4. Verify delegation executes
5. Test streaming response
6. Verify results appear in chat
7. Test error handling
8. Verify agent status updates
9. Test with different agent types
10. Check performance

Acceptance Criteria:
- Intent parsing is accurate
- Routing is correct
- Delegation executes
- Streaming works
- Errors are handled
- Performance is acceptable

issues.title: REQ-TECH-009: Implement Provider Bridge Integration
issues.parent: Phase 4: Multi-Provider Switching - Provider Detection & Hot-Swap
issues.description:
REQ-TECH-009: Provider Bridge Integration
Chat messages routed to non-Claude providers use the existing bridge modules:
- `bridges/openai_bridge.py` for ChatGPT
- `bridges/gemini_bridge.py` for Gemini
- `bridges/groq_bridge.py` for Groq
- `bridges/kimi_bridge.py` for KIMI
- `bridges/windsurf_bridge.py` for Windsurf

Test Steps:
1. Test ChatGPT bridge integration
2. Verify response comes from ChatGPT
3. Test Gemini bridge
4. Verify Gemini response
5. Test Groq, KIMI, Windsurf bridges
6. Verify each returns correct provider response
7. Test error handling for unavailable provider
8. Test switching between providers
9. Verify no response overlap
10. Check performance

Acceptance Criteria:
- All 6 bridges work
- Responses are accurate
- No cross-provider confusion
- Error handling works
- Performance is good

issues.title: REQ-TECH-010: Implement Configuration via Environment Variables
issues.parent: Phase 1: Foundation - Dashboard Server & Agent Status
issues.description:
REQ-TECH-010: Environment Variables
New environment variables (all optional, with sensible defaults):
- `DASHBOARD_WEB_PORT` (default: 8420) — Port for the web dashboard server
- `DASHBOARD_WS_PORT` (default: 8421) — Port for WebSocket connections (or same port via upgrade)
- `DASHBOARD_HOST` (default: 0.0.0.0) — Host to bind the dashboard server
- `DASHBOARD_AUTH_TOKEN` (none) — Bearer token for dashboard API authentication
- `DASHBOARD_CORS_ORIGINS` (default: *) — Allowed CORS origins

Test Steps:
1. Start server with default settings
2. Verify defaults are used
3. Set DASHBOARD_WEB_PORT=9999
4. Verify server listens on 9999
5. Set DASHBOARD_AUTH_TOKEN
6. Verify authentication required
7. Test with valid token
8. Test with invalid token
9. Set DASHBOARD_CORS_ORIGINS
10. Verify CORS headers

Acceptance Criteria:
- All env vars work
- Defaults are sensible
- Configuration is flexible
- No hardcoded values
- Documentation is clear

issues.title: REQ-TECH-011: Implement Authentication
issues.parent: Phase 1: Foundation - Dashboard Server & Agent Status
issues.description:
REQ-TECH-011: Authentication
If `DASHBOARD_AUTH_TOKEN` is set, all API endpoints and WebSocket connections require a valid bearer token. If not set, the dashboard is open (suitable for local development).

Test Steps:
1. Start server without auth token
2. Verify endpoints accessible without token
3. Set auth token
4. Verify endpoints require token
5. Test valid token
6. Test invalid token (401)
7. Test missing token header
8. Test in WebSocket upgrade
9. Test token in Authorization header
10. Check token validation is performed

Acceptance Criteria:
- Authentication is optional
- Auth works when configured
- No auth when not configured
- Token validation is correct
- WebSocket auth works
- Error messages are clear

issues.title: REQ-TECH-012: Implement Sandbox Compliance
issues.parent: Phase 1: Foundation - Dashboard Server & Agent Status
issues.description:
REQ-TECH-012: Sandbox Compliance
The dashboard server must operate within the existing security model:
- Bash commands executed through the chat are subject to the allowlist in `security.py`
- File operations are restricted to the project directory
- MCP tool calls go through the Arcade gateway with existing authorization

Test Steps:
1. Test bash command through chat
2. Verify allowlist is checked
3. Test unauthorized command
4. Verify command is blocked
5. Test file operation
6. Verify project directory restriction
7. Try to access outside project
8. Verify access denied
9. Test MCP tool call
10. Verify Arcade gateway auth

Acceptance Criteria:
- Allowlist is enforced
- File operations are restricted
- MCP calls go through Arcade
- No security bypass possible
- Errors are clear

issues.title: REQ-UI-001: Implement Panel Collapsibility
issues.parent: Phase 1: Foundation - Dashboard Server & Agent Status
issues.description:
REQ-UI-001: Panel Collapsibility
The left panel collapses to an icon bar on narrow viewports. The main chat panel takes full width.

Test Steps:
1. Load dashboard on desktop (1920x1080)
2. Verify left panel is visible
3. Resize to tablet width (768px)
4. Verify left panel collapses to icons
5. Click icon to expand panel
6. Verify panel expands
7. Click again to collapse
8. Resize to mobile width (375px)
9. Verify main panel uses full width
10. Test on actual mobile device

Acceptance Criteria:
- Collapsible on narrow viewports
- Icons are clear and clickable
- Panel expands/collapses smoothly
- Main panel uses available space
- No content is cut off

issues.title: REQ-UI-002: Implement Dark Mode Toggle
issues.parent: Phase 1: Foundation - Dashboard Server & Agent Status
issues.description:
REQ-UI-002: Dark Mode
The dashboard defaults to dark mode (consistent with terminal/IDE workflows). A toggle switches to light mode.

Test Steps:

2. Verify dark mode is default
3. Find dark mode toggle

10. Verify preference persists

Acceptance Criteria:

- Toggle switches themes
- Both themes are readable
- Preference persists
- Smooth transition

issues.title: QA-001: Expand Test Coverage for Core Modules
issues.description:

issues.title: QA-002: Address Technical Debt & TODOs
issues.description:
From QA_updates.md: Address Technical Debt.
Found 12 TODO/FIXME comments across 9 files indicating incomplete work.
Priority Files: daemon/control_plane.py (3 TODOs), scripts/daemon.py (2 TODOs).

Action Items:
1. Audit all TODO/FIXME comments
2. Prioritize by impact
3. Schedule resolution
4. Create sub-tasks for each

2. List all findings
3. Estimate effort
4. Assign to sprints
5. Verify completion

issues.title: QA-003: Reduce Code Duplication in Bridge Classes
issues.description:
From QA_updates.md: Reduce Code Duplication - Pattern Identified.
All bridge classes (openai_bridge.py, gemini_bridge.py, etc.) share similar client patterns.

Recommendation: Extract common interface:
Create bridges/base_bridge.py with abstract base class:
```python
class BaseBridge(ABC):
   @abstractmethod
   async def send_task(self, task: str) -> BridgeResponse
  
   @abstractmethod 
   def get_auth_info(self) -> dict[str, str]
```

Test Steps:
1. Extract common code
2. Create base_bridge.py
3. Update all bridges to inherit
4. Run tests
5. Verify no regression

Acceptance Criteria:
- Base class is clear
- All bridges inherit
- No code duplication
- Tests pass

issues.title: QA-004: Performance Optimizations for Bridges
issues.description:
From QA_updates.md: Performance Optimizations.
Subprocess Calls: Multiple subprocess.run() calls could be optimized with connection pooling.
Async Patterns: Some I/O operations in bridges could benefit from asyncio subprocess handling.
Caching: API responses and authentication tokens could be cached.

Test Steps:
1. Profile subprocess calls
2. Identify bottlenecks
3. Implement connection pooling
4. Add asyncio subprocess handling
5. Implement caching
6. Measure improvements

Acceptance Criteria:
- 15-25% improvement in I/O operations
- No behavior change
- Tests pass

issues.title: QA-005: Centralized Configuration Management
issues.description:
From QA_updates.md: Configuration Management.
Current: Environment variables scattered throughout.
Recommendation: Centralized configuration class:
```python
@dataclass
class AgentConfig:
   windsurf_mode: WindsurfMode
   timeout: int
   api_keys: dict[str, str]
```

Test Steps:
1. Create config.py
2. Define AgentConfig dataclass
3. Update all modules to use
4. Verify all env vars are handled
5. Test config loading

Acceptance Criteria:
- Single source of truth for config
- All env vars handled
- No hardcoded values
- Tests pass

issues.title: QA-006: Standardize Error Handling with Custom Exception Hierarchy
issues.description:
From QA_updates.md: Error Handling Standardization.
Good Foundation: Consistent error patterns exist
Enhancement: Create custom exception hierarchy:
```python
class AgentError(Exception): pass
class BridgeError(AgentError): pass
class SecurityError(AgentError): pass
```

Test Steps:

2. Define exception classes
3. Update error handling
4. Test exception catching
5. Verify error messages

Acceptance Criteria:

- All errors use custom classes

issues.title: QA-007: Type Safety Improvements

issues.title: QA-009: Add Structured Logging and Monitoring

issues.title: QA-010: Add Dependency Security Auditing
